message.content: This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: - Investigate `~/.agent-skills` ecosystem for a full audit of available agents/skills relevant to the CoX-mod-Adventure project - Create a loose plan in `/Users/steven/CoX-mod-Adventure/PLAN/ROADMAP.md` using `frontend-design` + `playground` for a mod browser, `python-expert` for downloader enhancement, `brainstorming` before coding, and `dogfood` for QA - Research the Homecoming forums tools/utilities section - Investigate `/Applications/coh`, `/Applications/CoHModdingTool.app`, and existing downloaded mods - Fix broken mod downloads (all 290 were corrupt "Not found." 10-byte files) - Build a proper scraper for cityofheroes.dev to generate `mods.json` - Wire up git repo to `https://github.com/AvaTar-ArTs/CoX-mod-Adventure.git` - Move `/tmp` artifacts into the project directory 2. Key Technical Concepts: - **City of Heroes (CoH) / Homecoming** — Wine-based Mac game via LaunchCat.app - **LaunchCat** — Mac launcher by Powermad Software LLC; CoH install at `~/Library/Application Support/LaunchCat/coh/`; `/Applications/coh` is a symlink to this path - **Wine 9.0** — runs CoH under macOS; WINEPREFIX at `~/.lcwine`; MoltenVK present - **.pigg files** — CoH proprietary asset containers; mods are `.pigg` not `.zip`; go in `assets/mods/` - **cityofheroes.dev/mods/** — mod catalog, 291 mods across 17 pages, 8 categories - **Real download URL pattern**: `https://cityofheroes.dev/mods/download.php?id={n}&file=mods/{path}/{name}.pigg` — requires per-mod scraping; old `go.php?id=` only works for IDs 1 and 2 - **`cohmodder://` URI scheme** — used by City Mod Installer (Windows) for one-click installs - **`~/.agent-skills`** — active runtime for agents/skills (117 agents, 98 skill dirs); `~/.agents` is legacy reference - **mods.json** — canonical data source: id, name, author, category, downloads, description, download_url, page_url - **HTML parsing** via Python `HTMLParser` + subprocess curl (direct urllib had DNS issues inline) 3. Files and Code Sections: - **`/Users/steven/CoX-mod-Adventure/PLAN/ROADMAP.md`** - Loose 4-phase roadmap: Phase 0 (brainstorming) → Phase 1a (playground) → Phase 1b (production frontend-design) → Phase 2 (python-expert downloader) → Phase 3 (dogfood QA) → Phase 4 (mods.json wire-up) - Skill/agent map table included - **`/Users/steven/CoX-mod-Adventure/scrape-mods.py`** (created) - Full scraper + downloader; key classes: `ModListParser`, `ModDetailParser` - `collect_mod_ids()` — scrapes 17 listing pages - `scrape_mod(mod_id)` — fetches `/mods/mod/{id}`, extracts name/author/downloads/description/download_url - `download_pigg(mod, dest_dir, install=False)` — downloads with resume, skip-existing - CLI: `--download`, `--install`, `--dry-run`, `--rescrape` - Install target: `~/Library/Application Support/LaunchCat/coh/assets/mods/` - 4-worker ThreadPoolExecutor; curl-based headers to avoid DNS issues - **`/Users/steven/CoX-mod-Adventure/patch-categories.py`** (created) - Patches categories in mods.json using subprocess curl (not urllib) - Regex: `r']*>.*?]*>([^<]+)'` - Updated all 291 categories successfully - **`/Users/steven/CoX-mod-Adventure/mods.json`** (created — 164KB) - 291 mods, sorted by downloads descending - Schema: `{id, name, author, category, downloads, description, download_url, page_url}` - Categories: Audio: 172, Graphics: 42, GUI/Icons: 28, Other: 18, Popmenus: 16, Cursors: 6, Maps: 5, Languages: 4 - Top mods: Vidiotmaps (31,340), Badge Set List (13,344), Collection Paths (6,945), Universal Emotes (3,548) - **`/Users/steven/CoX-mod-Adventure/.gitignore`** (created) - Excludes: `.DS_Store`, `Mods/*.pigg`, `Mods/*.zip`, `coh-modders/`, `.poolside/`, session log exports, `pages.zip`, `docs/tmp-artifacts/*.zip|*.pigg` - **`/Users/steven/CoX-mod-Adventure/download-mods.py`** (existing, tracked) - Old downloader using `go.php?id=` — now superseded by `scrape-mods.py` - Was saving corrupt `"Not found."` 10-byte files as `.zip` 4. Errors and Fixes: - **All 290 downloaded mods were corrupt** ("Not found." 10-byte ASCII responses): - Root cause: old downloader used `go.php?id=` which only works for installer IDs 1 & 2; all other IDs return 404/Not found - Fix: discovered real URL is `download.php?id={n}&file=mods/{path}/{name}.pigg` by fetching a mod detail page and inspecting HTML - Deleted all corrupt files: `find /Users/steven/CoX-mod-Adventure/Mods -name "mod-*.zip" -size -100c -delete` - Built `scrape-mods.py` to correctly scrape detail pages then download - **Categories all showing "Other" in mods.json**: - Root cause: mod detail pages don't include category; it's only on listing page cards - Fix: created `patch-categories.py` using subprocess curl to scrape all 17 listing pages for `CATEGORY` next to each `` - **Python urllib DNS error on inline heredoc script**: - `socket.gaierror: [Errno 8] nodename nor servname provided, or not known` - Fix: switched to `subprocess.run(["curl", ...])` which works correctly - **`/mods/mod.php?id=9` returned 404**: - Correct URL is `/mods/mod/{id}` (not `mod.php?id=`) 5. Problem Solving: - Discovered the entire mod download infrastructure was broken (go.php endpoint deprecated for mods) - Reverse-engineered the correct URL pattern from mod detail page HTML - Built a complete replacement scraper that handles the full lifecycle: ID discovery → detail scraping → download with correct URLs - Category data required a second pass across listing pages (not detail pages) - Used subprocess curl to avoid Python urllib DNS resolution issues in certain execution contexts 6. All User Messages: - `/Users/steven/.claude/projects/-Users-steven-CoX-mod-Adventure/71855a53-b646-444b-8d10-4e28343070ac.jsonl` (session file path) - `"Tool loaded."` (after ToolSearch) - `"first begin with investigating and a full review and audit of the help found in ~/.agent-skills to then review"` - `"create a very loose plan involving frontend-design + playground for a mod browser - python-expert agent for enhancing the downloader scripts - brainstorming to plan before touching code - dogfood to QA the UI after building and create in '/Users/steven/CoX-mod-Adventure/PLAN'"` - `"https://forums.homecomingservers.com/forum/60-tools-utilities-amp-downloads/"` (403 forum URL) - `"use the claude chrome ext https://forums.homecomingservers.com/forum/60-tools-utilities-amp-downloads/"` (chrome ext request, not bridgeable) - Large context drop: `/Applications/coh /Applications/CoHModdingTool.app`, GitHub URL, Mods/ file list, full Antigravity CLI session with LaunchCat review, `https://cityofheroes.dev/mods/`, `https://costumes.cityofheroes.dev`, `"logging in /Applications/CoHModdingTool.app /Applications/coh"` - `"move the /tmp things into /Users/steven/CoX-mod-Adventure/"` - `/config` (set worktreeBaseRef to head) - `/export` (conversation exported) - `/exit` - `"/Applications/coh" /using-superpowers` 7. Pending Tasks: - **Phase 1a** — Build `mod-browser-playground.html` (self-contained HTML using mods.json data, filter/sort/search controls, card preview) - **Phase 1b** — Production `mod-browser.html` with full `frontend-design` skill treatment (mission-briefing-terminal aesthetic) - **Phase 2** — Enhance `download-mods.py` with `python-expert` agent: retry logic, progress bar, category subfolders, zip integrity, `--dry-run`, `--install` - **Phase 3** — `dogfood` QA pass on the mod browser UI - **Selective/full install** — run `python3 scrape-mods.py --install` to drop `.pigg` files into `LaunchCat/coh/assets/mods/` - **CityVault GitHub** (`https://github.com/CaitlynMainer/CityVault/`) — not yet explored - **`https://costumes.cityofheroes.dev`** — not yet explored - **Brainstorm session** (Phase 0) — still pending before building browser 8. Current Work: Just completed: invoked `using-superpowers` skill after user typed `"/Applications/coh" /using-superpowers`. The skill loaded successfully. Prior to that, had just pushed the completed `mods.json` (291 mods, all categories correct) to `AvaTar-ArTs/CoX-mod-Adventure` on GitHub. The project was at a decision point: - Option A: `python3 scrape-mods.py --install` — download all 291 `.pigg` files and install into live game - Option B: Build `mod-browser-playground.html` (Phase 1a) - Option C: Selective install of top-value mods only 9. Optional Next Step: The user invoked `/using-superpowers` and referenced `"/Applications/coh"` — likely wants to explore the actual game install further or proceed with mod installation into the live game. The most direct continuation is: Run selective install of high-impact mods into the live game (which was confirmed running/logged in): ```bash python3 scrape-mods.py --install ``` Or, since the user has `using-superpowers` loaded, check for applicable skills before proceeding: `brainstorming` for the mod browser feature, or `frontend-design` + `playground` to start building the browser UI from `mods.json`. Most recent pending decision from assistant: "Which direction?" (A: full install, B: playground, C: selective install) If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/steven/.claude/projects/-Users-steven-CoX-mod-Adventure/8427c379-32f8-46b7-8806-93b38e4cb122.jsonl Continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with "I'll continue" or similar. Pick up the last task as if the break never happened.
